mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, causing immediate negative market repricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of contraction periods, or the signal shows no statistical edge during tightening regimes in a multivariate framework.

mechanism_narrative: When nominal policy rates outpace inflation growth, real interest rates turn positive and rise, tightening financial conditions, suppressing equity valuations and driving negative returns.
failure_condition: Rising real rates coincide with positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, which translates into sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of high-GDP-growth quarters, or rolling information coefficient drops below 0.01 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 signals labor market weakness and reduced household income, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of payroll decline months, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates healthy economic expansion without overheating, supporting positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over main effects in a nested F-test.
